subject: [PATCH v2 0/3] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Identified potential downsides of limiting writeback to a specific inode, which aligns with previous concerns. Suggested considering the impact on performance and target release.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Apr 08, 2026 at 02:45:32PM -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> > Assuming that looks OK, I'll probably send a v3. Original cover letter
> > from v1 follows:
> > 
> 
> Actually, that version regressed performance in a couple of cases.
> I think v2 is probably the best approach, on balance.

Limiting writeback to a specific inode has many downsides as I explained
before, so this is not surprising.

> Maybe we can get this into -next so that it can make v7.2?

If you want to target 7.2 we're not in any rush.  If you were trying to
say 7.1 we're way to close too the merge window.  We also haven't
heard from Jens at all, whom I'd really like to look over this.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Provided a comparison between the current patch and a previous whole-BDI flusher kernel, highlighting performance regressions in some cases.

2026-04-08.patch_summary: The patch aims to improve write performance by introducing RWF_DONTCACHE, but it has regressed in some cases.

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary: Ritesh pointed out that the current API gives a misleading impression of kicking writeback for only an inode's address space mapping, when in fact it starts writeback for all dirty pages on the respective bdi. He suggested exporting the symbol for wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi() and using it instead, or alternatively modifying filemap_kick_writeback_all(mapping, enum wb_reason) to better reflect its behavior.
reviews.raw_body:
This api gives a wrong sense that we are kicking writeback to write
dirty pages which belongs to only this inode's address space mapping.
But instead we are starting wb for everything on the respective bdi.

So instead why not just export symbol for wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi()
and use it instead?

If not, then IMO at least making it... 
   filemap_kick_writeback_all(mapping, enum wb_reason)

... might be better.

2026-04-09.patch_summary: This patch set improves write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by kicking the flusher thread for the superblock instead of initiating writeback directly. This change is based on benchmarking results that show a clear improvement over the status quo, particularly in cases with heavy write activity. The patch also includes a comparison of different IO modes for nfsd and highlights the poor performance of RWF_DONTCACHE with write-heavy workloads. The approach taken involves modifying the generic_write_sync function to kick the flusher thread, which is expected to better isolate the DONTCACHE writeback behavior and improve overall performance.
